extraction.one_line_summary: CHIMA Damiano won from a three-man break after 500+km cumulative breakaway time this Giro, forcing DENZ to lead out inside 1km while sprinter teams miscalculated closing gap by 25 meters.
tactical_patterns: BORA-hansgrohe and Israel Cycling Academy both committed to sprint trains but inexplicably stopped chasing with under 5km, allowing break to survive.
CHIMA refused to contribute in final kilometer forcing DENZ to lead out, positioning himself perfectly right before peloton arrived 25m behind.
Sprinter teams lost organization when GC teams unexpectedly came forward under 5km, creating chaos that killed their chase momentum definitively.
Breakaway riders attacking each other mid-stage (DENZ solo move) nearly killed the move but paradoxically tired peloton gave up when gap stayed under 1min.

rider_signals.DENZ Nico: negative: strongest in break but tactically naive, allowed himself to be forced into leading out sprint from 1km after doing solo pull earlier
rider_signals.CHIMA Damiano: positive: accumulated 500+km in breaks this Giro showing exceptional endurance capacity, then executed perfect positioning to steal sprint
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: BORA team stopped working with 5km despite numerical advantage, sprint train disintegrated when GC teams disrupted organization
rider_signals.CONSONNI Simone: negative: third place from peloton despite being framed as top-tier sprinter shows positioning failure in chaotic finale
